heading: Resolution of disputes

children.heading: .—General
children.label: a .—General
children.text: The corporation shall establish and maintain provisions in its constitution and bylaws for the swift and equitable resolution of disputes involving any of its members and relating to complaints of retaliation or the opportunity of an amateur athlete, coach, trainer, manager, administrator, or official to participate in the Olympic Games, the Paralympic Games, the Pan-American Games, the Parapan American Games, world championship competition, or other protected competition as defined in the constitution and bylaws of the corporation. In any lawsuit relating to the resolution of a dispute involving the opportunity of an amateur athlete to participate in the Olympic Games, the Paralympic Games, the Pan-American Games, or the Parapan American Games, a court shall not grant injunctive relief against the corporation within 21 days before the beginning of such games if the corporation, after consultation with the chair of the Athletes’ Advisory Council, has provided a sworn statement in writing executed by an officer of the corporation to such court that its constitution and bylaws cannot provide for the resolution of such dispute prior to the beginning of such games.

children.heading: Office of the Athlete Ombuds.—
children.label: b Office of the Athlete Ombuds.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The corporation shall hire and provide salary, benefits, and administrative expenses for an ombudsman and support staff for athletes.

children.label: A
children.text: provide independent advice to athletes at no cost about the applicable provisions of this chapter and the constitution and bylaws of the corporation, national governing bodies, international sports federations, the International Olympic Committee, the International Paralympic Committee, and the Pan-American Sports Organization, and with respect to the resolution of any dispute involving the opportunity of an amateur athlete to participate in the Olympic Games, the Paralympic Games, the Pan-American Games, the Parapan American Games, world championship competition or other protected competition as defined in the constitution and bylaws of the corporation;

children.label: B
children.text: assist in the resolution of athlete concerns;

children.label: i
children.text: the role, responsibility, authority, and jurisdiction of the Center; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the relative value of engaging legal counsel; and

children.label: D
children.text: report to the Athletes’ Advisory Council on a regular basis.

children.label: i
children.text: The Athletes’ Advisory Council shall provide the corporation’s executive director with the name of 1 qualified person to serve as ombudsman for athletes.

children.label: ii
children.text: The corporation’s executive director shall immediately transmit the name of such person to the corporation’s executive committee.

children.label: iii
children.text: The corporation’s executive committee shall hire or not hire such person after fully considering the advice and counsel of the Athletes’ Advisory Council.

children.heading: .—Vacancy
children.label: B .—Vacancy
children.text: If there is a vacancy in the position of the ombudsman for athletes, the nomination and hiring procedure set forth in this paragraph shall be followed in a timely manner.

children.label: i
children.text: the termination is carried out in accordance with the applicable policies and procedures of the corporation;

children.label: ii
children.text: the termination is initially recommended to the corporation’s executive committee by either the corporation’s executive director or by the Athletes’ Advisory Council; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the corporation’s executive committee fully considers the advice and counsel of the Athletes’ Advisory Council prior to deciding whether or not to terminate the employment of such individual.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Office of the Athlete Ombuds shall maintain as confidential any information communicated or provided to the Office of the Athlete Ombuds in confidence in any matter involving the exercise of the official duties of the Office of the Athlete Ombuds.

children.heading: .—Exception
children.label: B .—Exception
children.text: The Office of the Athlete Ombuds may disclose information described in subparagraph (A) as necessary to resolve or mediate a dispute, with the permission of the parties involved.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The ombudsman and the staff of the Office of the Athlete Ombuds shall not be compelled to testify or produce evidence in any judicial or administrative proceeding with respect to any matter involving the exercise of the duties of the Office of the Athlete Ombuds.

children.label: I
children.text: shall be confidential; and

children.label: aa
children.text: subject to discovery, subpoena, or any other means of legal compulsion; or

children.label: bb
children.text: admissible as evidence in a judicial or administrative proceeding.

children.label: i
children.text: applicable federally mandated reporting requirements;

children.label: ii
children.text: a felony personally witnessed by a member of the Office of the Athlete Ombuds;

children.label: iii
children.text: a situation, communicated to the Office of the Athlete Ombuds, in which an individual is at imminent risk of serious harm; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a congressional subpoena.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of the Empowering Olympic, Paralympic, and Amateur Athletes Act of 2020, the Office of the Athlete Ombuds shall develop and publish in the Federal Register a confidentiality and privacy policy consistent with this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: employees of the national governing bodies; and

children.label: II
children.text: employees of the corporation.

children.label: I
children.text: publish the policy developed under clause (i) on the internet website of the national governing body; and

children.label: II
children.text: communicate to amateur athletes the availability of the policy.

children.heading: .—Prohibition on retaliation
children.label: 5 .—Prohibition on retaliation
children.text: No employee, contractor, agent, volunteer, or member of the corporation shall take or threaten to take any action against an athlete as a reprisal for disclosing information to or seeking assistance from the Office of the Athlete Ombuds.

children.heading: .—Independence in carrying out duties
children.label: 6 .—Independence in carrying out duties
children.text: The board of directors of the corporation or any other member or employee of the corporation shall not prevent or prohibit the Office of the Athlete Ombuds from carrying out any duty or responsibility under this section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The corporation, the national governing bodies, or any officer, employee, contractor, subcontractor, or agent of the corporation or a national governing body may not retaliate against any protected individual as a result of any communication, including the filing of a formal complaint, by a protected individual or a parent or legal guardian of the protected individual relating to an allegation of physical abuse, sexual harassment, or emotional abuse.

children.heading: .—Disciplinary action
children.label: 2 .—Disciplinary action
children.text: If the corporation finds that an employee of the corporation or a national governing body has retaliated against a protected individual, the corporation or national governing body, as applicable, shall immediately terminate the employment of, or suspend without pay, such employee.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: With respect to a protected individual the corporation finds to have been subject to retaliation, the corporation may award damages, including damages for pain and suffering and reasonable attorney fees.

children.heading: .—Reimbursement from national governing body
children.label: B .—Reimbursement from national governing body
children.text: In the case of a national governing body found to have retaliated against a protected individual, the corporation may demand reimbursement from the national governing body for damages paid by the corporation under subparagraph (A).
